The largest upward price movement occurred on 2026-02-04, with an 8.1544% increase closing at 363.68 on a high volume day of 5,899,600 shares traded. This jump coincided with multiple positive earnings-related headlines, including Amgen's Q4 earnings and revenues beating estimates and the company setting a new 12-month high following strong earnings [1][2][3][4][5]. Another notable up day was on 2026-02-06, with a 4.4916% gain closing at 381.6753, which aligned with announcements of quarterly dividends payable in March 2026 [6][7][8]. The largest down day was on 2026-01-08, with a -3.3749% decline closing at 327.8383, though no specific driver was identified for this date. A second largest down day occurred on 2026-03-05 with a -3.077% move closing at 367.6. The highest volume day recorded was on 2025-12-19, with 13,465,700 shares traded and a modest 0.9124% price increase, but no direct news driver was linked to this spike. Overall, the price movements and volume changes appear to be influenced by earnings reports, dividend announcements, and some legal and patent litigation updates, though some down days lack clear associated news events.
